channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Pattern-enhanced Resonant Soft X-ray Scattering for Operando monitoring of electrochemical solid-liquid interfaces
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 21 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69852-9

Understanding interfaces at sub-nanometer scales is vital for complex chemical reactions. Here, the authors advance Pattern-enhanced Resonant Soft X-ray Scattering, enabling efficient operando probing of chemical and structural dynamics at solid-liquid interfaces.
item.dc:title: Pattern-enhanced Resonant Soft X-ray Scattering for Operando monitoring of electrochemical solid-liquid interfaces
item.dc:creator: Haoyi Li
Kas Andrle
Qi Zhang
Isvar A. Cordova
Yao Yang
Zhengxing Peng
Feipeng Yang
Guillaume Freychet
Scott Dhuey
Alexander Hexemer
Brett A. Helms
Weilun Chao
Bruno La Fontaine
Ricardo Ruiz
Jinghua Guo
Wanli Yang
Junko Yano
Cheng Wang

item.title: A synergistic design model for ultrathin broadband microwave absorbers using electromagnetic frequency dispersion coefficients
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 21 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69591-x

The authors introduce the Electromagnetic Frequency Dispersion Coefficient (EFDC), a unified parameter linking material properties to microwave absorption. It enables ultra-thin, broadband absorption with record performance and stable operation across temperatures, simplifying the design of advanced absorbing materials.
item.dc:title: A synergistic design model for ultrathin broadband microwave absorbers using electromagnetic frequency dispersion coefficients
item.dc:creator: Haoxu Si
Yi Zhang
Mu Li
Zehui Chai
Jingwei Zhang
Cuiping Li
Chunhong Gong

item.title: Heterogeneous weakly coupled polar nanoclusters enabling superior high-temperature capacitive energy storage
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 21 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69631-6

Ceramic capacitors face challenges in energy storage performance at high temperatures, restricting their applications. Here the high-performance BaTiO3-based capacitors, as a paradigm, are designed by constructing weakly coupled polar nanoclusters.
item.dc:title: Heterogeneous weakly coupled polar nanoclusters enabling superior high-temperature capacitive energy storage
item.dc:creator: Qibin Yuan
Binglong Zheng
Ying Lin
Haibo Yang
Da Li
Tao Lei
Jinming Guo
Wen Gong
Yingchun Niu
Fang-Zhou Yao
Ke Wang
